nameMeanings.name: Trie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southeastern): from Middle English tre, treo, treu, trew, trow ‘tree’ (Old English trēo(w)). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived by a prominent tree, or toponymic, from a place so named, such as Tree, Trew, True, and Trow in Devon. See also Trewin .

variants.name: Treen
meanings: English: from Middle English tren(e), tre(a)wen ‘trees’, the weak plural form of tre ‘tree’ (see Tree ). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived by a group of trees, or toponymic, from a place so named, such as Trewyn in Holsworthy (Devon), Train in Wembury (Devon), Traine in Modbury (Devon), or the lost Trehane in Calstock (Cornwall).

variants.name: Trewin
meanings: Cornish (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from Trewin in Sheviock (Cornwall), Trewin in Lanteglos, near Camborne (Cornwall), and perhaps also from one or more of the places in Cornwall called Trewen. The placenames probably derive from Middle Cornish tre ‘estate, farmstead’ + guyn ‘white’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Trie Chaumont-Aquillon was born about 1112, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Dreux De Chaumont, was 29 and his mother, Jehanne l'Aiguillonne De Trie, was 28. He had at least 4 daughters with Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT. He died in 1148, in Jerusalem, Palestine, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Trie Chaumont-Aquillon est né environ 1112, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Dreux De Chaumont, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Jehanne l'Aiguillonne De Trie, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 filles avec Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT. Il est décédé en 1148, à Jerusalem, Palestine, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Trie Chaumont-Aquillon um 1112 in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Dreux De Chaumont 29 und seine Mutter Jehanne l'Aiguillonne De Trie 28. Er hatte mit Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb 1148 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Jerusalem, Palästina.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Trie Chaumont-Aquillon è nato il circa 1112 nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Dreux De Chaumont, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Jehanne l'Aiguillonne De Trie, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figlie con Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT. Morì nel 1148, nel Jerusalem, Palestina, all'età di 37 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Trie Chaumont-Aquillon(이)가 대략 1112년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Dreux De Chaumont(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Jehanne l'Aiguillonne De Trie(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT(와)과 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1148년 팔레스타인 제루사렘에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhas com Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT. Ele faleceu em 1148, em Jerusalem, Palestina, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Trie Chaumont-Aquillon родился около 1112 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Dreux De Chaumont было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Jehanne l'Aiguillonne De Trie, 28. У него было по крайней мере 4 дочери от Marguerite DE GISORS DE CHAUMONT. Он умер в 1148 г. в местности джирсеилем, Палестина в возрасте 37 лет.
